industry_leaders.name: Thomas May Bespoke
industry_leaders.standout: Dark, atmospheric photography with luxury editorial feel — more interiors magazine than joinery site
industry_leaders.layout: Image-heavy, portfolio-led, full-width backgrounds, alternating text/image blocks
industry_leaders.colours: Dark and sophisticated — deep neutrals, near-black backgrounds, restrained highlights
industry_leaders.typography: Mixed serif/sans — serif headings for elegance, sans body for readability
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Categorised galleries (Kitchens, Bathrooms, Bedrooms, Furniture, Windows, Doors) plus dedicated case studies

industry_leaders.name: Grovewood Joinery
industry_leaders.standout: Gold accent (#aa9a5d) against black is immediately distinctive and luxurious
industry_leaders.layout: Clean, modular, hero imagery with vertical-flow content sections
industry_leaders.colours: Black (#000) with warm gold (#aa9a5d) and white text
industry_leaders.typography: Mixed — Karla sans for nav/body, serif-style headings
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Portfolio grid, embedded client testimonials from architects and designers
industry_leaders.feeling: Premium craft, positioned for architect collaboration

industry_leaders.name: Tates Joinery
industry_leaders.standout: Cleanest, most modern design — deep navy (#0a1b61) with warm beige (#ecece7) is distinctive
industry_leaders.layout: Minimalist with ample whitespace, full-width grid, generous spacing
industry_leaders.colours: Deep navy/indigo, warm beiges, muted taupe — premium and elegant
industry_leaders.typography: Montserrat exclusively, weights from thin to bold, contemporary
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Gallery with featured imagery, testimonials as bold pull-quotes

industry_leaders.name: James Smith Furniture
industry_leaders.standout: Most artisan feeling — award-winning Edinburgh cabinetmaker, Instagram feed integration makes it feel alive
industry_leaders.colours: Neutral — whites, blacks, greyscale. Wood tones through photography, not design elements
industry_leaders.typography: Mixed serif/sans — sans nav with serif body text, elegant contrast
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Integrated gallery by category (Work, Commissions, Workshop) plus live Instagram feed showing process

industry_leaders.name: SPLINTR
industry_leaders.standout: Most design-studio feeling — 'WE CREATE THE SPACES PEOPLE TALK ABOUT' — bold, confident positioning
industry_leaders.layout: Portfolio-led, image-centric, minimalist, full-width slideshow hero
industry_leaders.colours: Sophisticated neutrals — white and dark grey, photography does all colour work
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Portfolio gallery, visual impact over detailed case studies

industry_leaders.name: Wessex Joinery
industry_leaders.standout: Best service categorisation — 7 clear product types immediately visible, storytelling case studies
industry_leaders.layout: Hierarchical, service-focused, hero navigation into modular card grid
industry_leaders.colours: White/cream backgrounds, dark neutrals, heritage feel
industry_leaders.typography: Mixed serif/sans — traditional serif headings, clean sans body
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Case study narratives with read-more links — storytelling approach

industry_leaders.name: Stafford Joinery
industry_leaders.standout: 40+ year heritage combined with modern CAD imagery — tradition meets technology
industry_leaders.layout: Portfolio-led, full-width background images, modular service cards
industry_leaders.colours: Dark backgrounds with gold/cream accents (#f6cc8e), neutral greys
industry_leaders.typography: Sans-serif only — Questrial uppercase headers, Inter body, generous letter-spacing
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Large background images in service cards, masonry gallery, 8 service categories

industry_leaders.name: Gowercroft Joinery
industry_leaders.standout: Best multi-audience approach — dedicated paths for Architects, Construction Professionals, Homeowners
industry_leaders.layout: Modern, content-rich, hero sections with card-based product layouts
industry_leaders.colours: Warm, earthy with green accents (sustainability), gold/amber for awards
industry_leaders.typography: Mixed serif/sans — sans for nav/body, serif for heritage headings
industry_leaders.work_presentation: Case studies with before/after narrative, context-challenge-solution format
industry_leaders.feeling: Premium craftsman meets corporation, Made in Britain emphasis

adjacent_premium.name: Dinesen
adjacent_premium.transferable: Heritage narrative, project photography in architectural context, wood grain as visual hero, 17+ atmospheric case studies
adjacent_premium.layout: White backgrounds, vertical flow, enormous whitespace
adjacent_premium.colours: White, dark text, natural wood tones as only colour
adjacent_premium.feeling: Quiet authority. Timeless. The wood speaks for itself.

adjacent_premium.name: Thuma
adjacent_premium.transferable: Alternating rhythm of studio/lifestyle/close-up shots, poetic headline copy, Japanese joinery references
adjacent_premium.layout: Modular grid, generous whitespace, mega-menu with product imagery
adjacent_premium.colours: White, charcoal, wood tones from photography only

adjacent_premium.name: Barrett Made
adjacent_premium.transferable: Portfolio-first homepage, in-progress shots alongside finished work, ultra-light font weight (100), three-link navigation
adjacent_premium.typography: Uppercase headers with extremely light weight — sparse and architectural
adjacent_premium.feeling: Architectural precision. Design reflects the craft.

adjacent_premium.name: Christian Troels
adjacent_premium.transferable: Rejecting the rustic default, positioning joinery alongside contemporary design, thumbnail grid, minimal text
adjacent_premium.layout: Clean grid, generous whitespace, neutral palette
adjacent_premium.colours: Whites, greys, blacks with warm wood tones from photography
adjacent_premium.feeling: Contemporary craft — bridges maker and designer.

design_forward.name: Martinelli Venezia
design_forward.transferable: Gallery-over-commerce approach, photography does 90% of communication, four-item nav only, enormous negative space
design_forward.layout: Grid-based but not rigid, images breathe
design_forward.feeling: Confident silence. Work speaks for itself.

design_forward.name: Aesop
design_forward.transferable: Design system from physical materials (grain/tools/workshop palette for joiner), calm transitions, educational content embedded naturally, digital mirrors physical experience

key_patterns.colours: Dark palettes (black/navy/charcoal) with gold/warm accents signal premium in joinery. Neutral palettes with wood tones from photography also work. Avoid: bright blues, greens, multi-colour schemes.
key_patterns.typography: Mixed serif/sans is the premium signal — serif headings for craft/heritage, sans body for modern readability. Best sites use tight tracking on large headings.
key_patterns.work_presentation: Case studies beat galleries. Show work in context (the kitchen in the home, not the kitchen in isolation). Include in-progress shots and material close-ups.
key_patterns.navigation: 3-4 items maximum. The best sites lead with the work, not with services lists.
key_patterns.whitespace: The more space around content, the more premium it feels. Every top site uses generous padding.
key_patterns.photography: Photography IS the design. Neutral site chrome lets the wood and craftsmanship be the visual interest.
key_patterns.heritage: Years of experience always prominent. Track record builds trust in trade contexts.
